rationale: The two paper notes show strong conceptual overlap: the citing study centers on token redemption effort (e.g., promo code/CAPTCHA) in discounts, while the cited study examines pseudo-free offers that require nonmonetary costs such as time/effort and their impact on acceptance. Because both address how small nonmonetary effort/cost in promotional offers changes consumer response, the cited paper is plausibly a substantive theoretical backdrop rather than a purely incidental mention.
